best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback/reclaim continuation remains the main revenue engine despite tail heat.
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long produced $2299.7 over 30 trades, 32.9% of latest PnL, 184.7 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Broad pruning is dangerous because structure score 3 and weak-distance buckets still contain substantial winners.

best_edges.edge: Charm-pin pullback is a fast, repeatable scalp edge, but not a clean risk bucket.
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long produced $1324.4 over 36 trades with 5.0 minute median hold and average MAE -75.1. Prior charm-pin tail box work improved the leader from run_011 to run_014 without throughput damage, so preserve the core while testing only narrow tail-state splits.

best_edges.edge: 1DTE breakout fallback is profitable insurance when 0DTE is unavailable.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced 20 trades and $1304.0 in VALID_1DTE_ONLY state. Prior direct quality tightening hurt score by -355 to -594 and reduced PnL, so do not simply harden this profile.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze continuation has the cleanest efficiency among multi-trade VALID_0DTE active profiles.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long produced $477.7 over 5 trades, 424.9 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE only -113.9. The recent upside-capture experiment improved score and net profit, though it worsened worst MAE through cross-profile effects, so expand cautiously.

best_edges.edge: Charm-wall breakaway is small-sample but high-value continuation.
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long produced $1120.6 over 7 trades, 240.5 PnL/exposure hour, median TP 12.0, no slow trades. Prior structure tightening reduced score and PnL, so avoid further hard gating until more sample accumulates.

broken_edges.evidence: Dormant/disabled active profiles generated zero trades in the current evidence set. Enabling all would add router noise without a defined hypothesis.
broken_edges.problem: Several active router profiles are dead or intentionally disabled in this sample.
broken_edges.profile: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long

broken_edges.evidence: run_018 pullback TP/VWAP re-expansion increased exposure by 22.38 hours, created one slow >=12h trade, worsened worst MAE from -527 to -2026.5, and dropped score by -1731 despite small net-profit gain.
broken_edges.problem: Pullback re-expansion via wider VWAP/TP is a proven tail-risk failure mode.

broken_edges.evidence: 1DTE local quality gates in run_007/run_008 cut 6-7 trades, reduced net profit by $317-$558, worsened drawdown, and did not solve the global tail.
broken_edges.problem: Direct 1DTE hardening removes profitable insurance faster than it reduces risk.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE contributed only $162.8 over 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades had 52.6 minute median hold, -210.6 average MAE, and only 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, but sample size is too small for global conclusions.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback state is under-sampled and uneven; it should not be mixed into broad optimizer batches.

broken_edges.evidence: Extended-VWAP bad-MAE signatures cover all 24 bad rows, but protected extended-VWAP winners total $720.8 with median MAE -14.45 and median hold 1.18 minutes. Blunt VWAP-distance or structure filters would block edge trades.
broken_edges.problem: The tail state overlaps heavily with profitable extension winners.

market_regime_read: The sample is a strongly favorable long-only continuation/pullback regime: 105 completed trades, 100% realized win rate, $6987.6 PnL, no realized losses, no slow >=12h trades, and 12.5% calendar exposure. The risk-adjusted problem is hidden heat, not directionality: worst MAE deteriorated to -638.75 versus the leader baseline -527 while average MAE stayed near -104. The path data is the key warning: only 48.6% went positive before max adverse, only 8.6% made MFE before MAE, and only 1.0% touched TP before MAE. The optimizer should treat this as a recovery-prone, no-stop regime where global filters can destroy profitable recoveries; research should focus on regime splits and tail-state classifiers rather than broad entry tightening.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates profitable pullback continuation from deep-heat pullbacks that show weak structure and no early progress.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Config-only proxies may block the same weak-structure pullbacks that currently generate large PnL; code needs early-path or state-normalized features to avoid over-pruning.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Per-entry ES structure distances, VWAP acceleration/slope, rolling-high age, first-observed path movement, early MFE/MAE, profile, and option data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entries with structure score <=3, negative higher-low or higher-high distance, low/negative VWAP acceleration, and delayed first profit.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% of pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -638.75 toward <= -527 and improving total risk-adjusted score.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Routes 1DTE fallback into breakout versus pullback states instead of hardening the existing breakout profile.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May be unreachable or cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades if router ordering/profile matching is too broad.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries with ES pullback/reclaim state, option map availability, forward DEX ratio, call/put imbalance, and existing breakout_long_1dte trade outcomes.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1304, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.7, and total net profit remains >=98% of leader baseline.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Tests whether a dormant valid-0DTE continuation concept can harvest call-flow/charm breakout state without disturbing pullback/charm-pin cores.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant activation could add unreachable rules or steal high-quality trades from squeeze/charm-wall profiles; sample must be isolated.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Sample Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE option/charm/delta state, call-volume imbalance, ES breakout state, profile attribution, and cannibalization versus squeeze_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Positive charm/delta continuation with ES above VWAP and accepted upside option context where existing squeeze/charm-wall profiles are sparse.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 trades, positive independent PnL, PnL/exposure hour >250, worst MAE better than -150, and total leader PnL retained >=98%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Uses existing dormant pin-avoid concepts to split the overlap between extended-VWAP winners and charm/pin tail-risk rows.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Pin-avoid may become a blunt anti-extension filter and block protected extended-VWAP winners unless activation is profile-scoped and measured by cannibalization.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pin-Avoid Holdout Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE charm/pin distance, rolling-high rejection, VWAP extension, charm wall distance, profile attribution, and protected extended-VWAP winner overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Entries near pin/charm-wall contexts with high VWAP extension and rejection features where current active profiles still allow high-MAE recoveries.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: New profile independently positive, reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE, preserves >=95% of protected fast extended-VWAP winner PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates clean mean-reversion/pin fade from the disabled late-day pin fade concept without changing core morning continuation routing.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Current sample has no active late_day_pin_fade trades; activation may be unreachable or introduce low-frequency noise.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Late-Day Pin Avoid Mean-Reversion Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Time-of-day, pin distance, gamma regime, rolling-high rejection, VWAP distance, and option expiration proximity.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Late session VALID_0DTE pin/charm-wall proximity where continuation profiles are disabled or weak and mean-reversion state is explicit.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Minimum 5 trades over expanded sample, positive PnL, average MAE below active-profile median, and no degradation in core VALID_0DTE profiles.

next_experiments.config_bias: Research a router/profile split rather than a broad filter: preserve pullback_long throughput, but isolate weak-structure pullbacks with delayed early progress into a companion risk bucket or profile-specific gate. Avoid widening TP/VWAP after run_018 failure.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether pullback_long is two regimes: high-throughput continuation versus deep-heat recovery.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The -638.75 worst tail and the -403.2 tail share weak/mixed ES structure and poor early path behavior, while broad structure buckets remain profitable. A split can improve tail MAE without starving the pullback edge.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Split Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total risk-adjusted score, pullback_long worst MAE, pullback PnL retention >=90%, no slow >=12h trades.
next_experiments.risk: Current legal config keys may not express early-path delay; a blunt structure score increase is likely to remove profitable winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte active and avoid repeating min-score/slope hardening. Test a companion routing/profile idea for 1DTE pullback state, measuring cannibalization rather than trying to make the breakout profile stricter.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether VALID_1DTE_ONLY has separate breakout and pullback regimes.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The 1DTE profile is too profitable to prune, but its average MAE is high. A pullback companion may reduce average heat while preserving the insurance PnL that direct hardening destroyed.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Profile Discovery
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1304, 1DTE average MAE better than -123.7, total net profit >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Small 20-trade sample and all winners; router overlap could simply relabel trades without improving risk.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use a dormant valid-0DTE activation as an isolated sample builder. Prefer one dormant concept at a time with a full router replacement and minimal profile tuning; do not activate multiple dormant profiles in one run.
next_experiments.goal: Find a new continuation regime that can add high-efficiency trades without disturbing established pullback/charm-pin edges.
next_experiments.hypothesis: call_wall_acceptance_long and squeeze_long show very high efficiency in tiny samples; a dormant delta/charm breakout concept may capture adjacent flow-driven continuation states.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Delta-Charm Breakout Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile reaches >=5 trades, positive PnL, PnL/exposure hour >250, worst MAE better than -150, total PnL retained >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Single-trade/high-efficiency evidence can overfit; activation may steal trades from squeeze_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.

next_experiments.config_bias: Treat the extended-VWAP rejection signature as a diagnostic for a split, not as a broad cap. If translated config-only, only nudge narrow miss thresholds and require protected-winner overlap review.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce hidden heat from extended-VWAP/rejection states without killing fast extension winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: All bad-MAE rows are covered by the extended-VWAP near-miss family, but clean extended-VWAP winners are a real edge; only a profile-aware refinement can separate them.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Tail-State Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -527, total PnL retained >=97%, protected extended-VWAP winner PnL retained >=90%.
next_experiments.risk: Raising max RSI slope or lowering pullback thresholds too far may block protected fast winners and reduce the core edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Hold the no-0DTE fallback set stable unless running a dedicated stale-data experiment. If tested, isolate stale/expired states and avoid changing VALID_0DTE profiles in the same run.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether stale/expired fallback routing deserves a separate defensive profile policy.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE is positive but inefficient; breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades show poor heat/exposure, while EXPIRED_0DTE had one strong winner. The state may need separate routing but sample is too small.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Fallback Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL non-negative, fallback average MAE improves, VALID_0DTE PnL unchanged, total score no worse than leader by more than noise.
next_experiments.risk: Only 6 fallback trades; any conclusion is highly unstable and can overfit rare data-state events.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze charm_wall_breakaway_long thresholds after the failed structure-gate test; only collect more sample or test a separate dormant adjacent concept. Do not tighten higher-high/higher-low again.
next_experiments.goal: Protect a high-PnL, low-sample continuation edge while exploring adjacent regimes separately.
next_experiments.hypothesis: charm_wall_breakaway_long is efficient and profitable, but prior structure tightening reduced score; the correct next step is sample expansion or adjacent-profile discovery, not hard gating.
next_experiments.name: Charm-Wall Edge Preservation Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile PnL/exposure remains >200, total net profit >=98%, no worsening in worst MAE.
next_experiments.risk: Only 7 trades; aggressive optimization can remove one large winner and distort full-run score.

research_notes: The next optimizer batch should not chase the latest run_019 net-profit improvement blindly because it came with worse worst MAE and drawdown than the leader base. The most useful research direction is regime discovery: pullback_long and charm_pin_pullback_long are profitable but tail-heavy; breakout_long_1dte is profitable but heat-heavy and should be split, not tightened; squeeze/call-wall states are high-efficiency but under-sampled. Prior evidence strongly rejects broad pullback TP/VWAP re-expansion, direct 1DTE hardening, and call-wall loosening as mixed batches. Dormant activation is allowed only as an isolated router/profile experiment with explicit cannibalization checks.
risk_controls: Use run_001/005/014/017 leader metrics as the acceptance baseline, not run_019, because run_019 worsened worst MAE to -638.75 and drawdown to 0.5%.
Reject any experiment that creates slow >=12h trades or materially increases exposure hours without a proportional score gain.
Require total net profit retention >=98% for exploratory config-only runs unless the explicit objective is tail-risk reduction with clear worst-MAE improvement.
Protect the 65 fast/low-MAE winner fingerprints; any entry filter that blocks many of them needs a much stronger MAE improvement than currently shown.
Do not combine dormant activation, fallback routing changes, and core-profile tail gates in one run; attribution would be unreadable.
For dormant profile probes, activate only one dormant concept per run and include cannibalization accounting versus active profiles.
